125938.explanation: We believe that officials should be elected or appointed based on quality, experience, and expertise, not based on a mandatory gender distribution.

125939.explanation: A local referendum gives residents an extra voice about their neighborhood or city and increases engagement. It leads to decisions with broad support. Of course, IBV advocates for your interests as a resident.

125940.explanation: When residents take over management of a sports hall or park, they feel more responsible. They often know well what their neighborhood needs and can organize this faster and more tailor-made than the municipality itself.

125942.explanation: We agree with this, but a farm shop may only sell products from its own business. This prevents it from becoming a resale shop and keeps competition with other local shops fair.

125944.explanation: We believe employment and economic development are important. New distribution centers can create jobs, strengthen local business activity, and improve goods accessibility, provided they are well integrated into the landscape and traffic is taken into account.

125945.explanation: Residents have clearly stated they no longer want new long-term asylum reception. Therefore, IBV continues to fight against the unrealistic targets of the Distribution Act. Safety and housing opportunities for our own residents come first.

125946.explanation: The responsibility lies with the employers. The municipality should allow this with clear rules but does not have to support the implementation.

125947.explanation: As far as IBV is concerned, even more trees will be planted.

125951.explanation: We believe this should not be free by default. Otherwise, everyone pays for a service that not everyone needs.

125953.explanation: Companies themselves are responsible for making adjustments to their buildings.

125954.explanation: Farmers should be able to build homes on their land themselves, in addition to the farm house: a home for business succession or a caregiver's home.

125955.explanation: We believe the municipality should continue to support this.

125956.explanation: We believe the municipality should not provide additional funding to the local broadcaster. Free and independent media should be able to organize themselves.

125958.explanation: It stimulates creativity and development. By offering it to every child, we give all children the same chance to discover their talent.

125959.explanation: Sports clubs are important for our villages and neighborhoods. If finances are tight, the municipality should be able to help so that sports remain accessible to everyone and clubs can continue to exist.

125963.explanation: Additional camera surveillance is not necessary. We value privacy. However, mobile cameras can be temporarily deployed during emergencies, but only if truly necessary for safety.

125964.explanation: Help must be prompt and professional via regional shelter and care partners. At the same time, we want to focus mainly on prevention and good early assistance. We want to minimize the need for a women's shelter by stopping and preventing violence earlier.

125968.explanation: IBV agrees with this, but only with clear rules. Due to the housing shortage, some vacation homes can temporarily serve as a residential address, carefully and step-by-step. Our goal is more housing opportunities for residents, not unnecessary price increases. The tourism sector must remain protected.

125969.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, building should be possible. New construction may also extend to village boundaries, as long as it is well planned and fits the village. We want to allow growth, but in a smart and careful way.

125970.explanation: If you receive social assistance, it is fair to try to give something back to society. This gives satisfaction. The work must be feasible and suit what someone can do.

125971.explanation: Residents with a low income often already face financial difficulties. Waiving municipal taxes is an important safety net to prevent people from falling deeper into trouble. The municipality must continue to provide this support carefully and fairly.

125974.explanation: In Voorne aan Zee, parking spaces are already scarce. If too many spots are reserved only for charging, parking becomes harder for residents and visitors. We prefer smart solutions, like charging on private premises or flexible charging points, without putting pressure on parking.

125976.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is important. People should be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them. This leads to more trust and better care, because a person feels understood and respected.

125979.explanation: Tourists and visitors use our roads, beaches, villages and facilities. It is fair that they contribute financially. Tourist tax ensures costs are not borne only by residents and local businesses. For that reason, this tax should not be lowered.

125985.explanation: Voorne aan Zee already has a maximum of two coffeeshops. More are not needed. By blocking new locations, we maintain control over the situation and protect peace and safety in our villages and neighborhoods.

125987.explanation: More tall residential buildings can be built in Voorne aan Zee, but only in places where flats already exist. This keeps our villages balanced and ensures new construction fits with the existing streetscape and environment.

125988.explanation: Because of the housing shortage, the municipality should allow extra housing solutions. Renting out single rooms can help, for example for students or starters who do not yet need a whole house. This provides more opportunities to find a place to live faster.

125989.explanation: Parking spaces in the centers of Voorne aan Zee are scarce. Extra spots are needed so residents, entrepreneurs and visitors can park their cars more easily. Good accessibility starts with sufficient parking space.

125992.explanation: We agree with the construction of the Patatweg because good and safe access to businesses is important. We understand some residents have concerns about the landscape and location. The municipality must do this carefully, in an open dialogue and with respect for the surroundings.

125995.explanation: IBV fully agrees with this. New wind turbines in Hellevoetsluis are undesirable. They do not fit the open and peaceful character of Voorne aan Zee. For us, health, safety and protecting our living environment come first.

125938.explanation: Quality always comes first. With equal suitability, diversity can be considered.

125939.explanation: The municipal council is elected by residents to make decisions on their behalf and is closely connected to society. Moreover, referenda reduce solutions to a black-and-white choice and slow down decision-making. VVD prefers other forms of citizen involvement, such as participation.

125940.explanation: VVD advocates less government interference. VVD stimulates residents’ own initiatives and wants to enable autonomy with clear conditions to ensure quality remains guaranteed.

125942.explanation: Farmers should have space to undertake activities, for example with a shop or campsite. This must fit within the zoning plan.

125944.explanation: New distribution centers must be assessed per location. Economic opportunities, traffic congestion, landscape, infrastructure and living environment are considered before allowing establishment.

125945.explanation: The current shelter on the asylum ship must close as agreed. At the same time, implementation of the Distribution Act and exploration of suitable reception forms remain necessary to meet legal targets.

125946.explanation: Entrepreneurs who employ foreign workers provide suitable housing outside residential neighborhoods, preferably on their own premises.

125947.explanation: VVD opposes a strict obligation that a new tree must be planted for every tree cut down. The municipality tries to replant as many trees as possible when trees are cut.

125951.explanation: Users of the service pay themselves so that the burden does not have to increase for all residents. Independently bringing bulky waste to the recycling center should remain free of charge.

125953.explanation: Accessibility is important and the municipality ensures that its own buildings are accessible. However, entrepreneurs must bear the costs themselves. This is not a task of the municipality.

125954.explanation: Farmers can already build a house on their land if this fits within the zoning plan and applicable rules.

125955.explanation: It is not the municipality’s task to pay for sports subscriptions for adults. For children whose parents cannot afford this, the municipality can support through the youth fund, by achieving low costs with clubs, and by providing free sports facilities in new neighborhoods.

125956.explanation: The municipality does not need to provide extra financial support to the local broadcaster. They already receive subsidies from, among others, the national government and the municipality. Extra money is not needed.

125958.explanation: The municipality does not have to automatically subsidize music lessons for every primary school child. However, there are schemes such as the Participation Fund and the Child Package to enable participation for children from low-income families.

125959.explanation: Sports clubs receive subsidies from the municipality, but directly stepping in during financial problems does not encourage responsible use of these funds.

125963.explanation: Safety is a top priority for the VVD. CCTV surveillance helps maintain public order, increases the chance of catching offenders, and contributes to the feeling of safety, especially in high-risk areas.

125964.explanation: Violence against women is unacceptable. Voorne aan Zee does not need to establish its own women's shelter because regional facilities already exist where victims can turn to.

125968.explanation: Temporary residence in holiday parks should be partially possible without losing sight of the essential tourism sector. This requires a tailored approach.

125969.explanation: New construction outside the existing village boundaries must remain possible to solve the housing shortage, provided the rural character of the municipality is preserved.

125970.explanation: Social services help people get back on their feet. Social assistance recipients should do socially useful work to keep active, develop skills, and have a springboard to work and social participation.

125971.explanation: Everyone uses municipal facilities and should contribute accordingly. Therefore, waiver of municipal taxes for low-income residents should not be automatic.

125974.explanation: Electric cars must be able to be charged, but the municipality cannot place extra charging stations everywhere due to high parking pressure. Standards have been set per area and shortages should be assessed per situation.

125976.explanation: The VVD is positive about patients' freedom of choice and autonomy but believes that the municipality should not play a role in attracting healthcare providers based on religion, culture, or beliefs.

125979.explanation: The tourism sector is important for the municipality. Tourist tax is set so that the local economy and attractiveness for tourists are maintained without placing extra burdens on residents.

125985.explanation: The VVD stands for market forces. The establishment of new coffee shops by entrepreneurs should therefore not be prohibited, but safety is crucial.

125987.explanation: The VVD supports housing construction and flow, but tall apartment buildings must fit their surroundings, like the buildings currently in Struytse Hoeck. In our villages and neighborhoods this is often less appropriate, so a tailored approach is important.

125988.explanation: Renting out individual rooms is prohibited in the municipality to prevent overcrowding and unwanted concentrations of labor migrants. The VVD wants to make exceptions for special initiatives, such as young people living with elderly people.

125989.explanation: There are serious parking problems in some places. The VVD believes sufficient parking must be available close to homes and facilities.

125992.explanation: The nuisance from truck traffic to and from Farm Frites must be reduced. The construction of the 'fries road' can relieve traffic around Kickersbloem 3. Its realization should be done in consultation with Farm Frites about financing.

125995.explanation: The VVD opposes new onshore wind turbine projects.

125938.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality.

125939.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue is very important, a referendum can be a fair and good way to reach a decision. This way, we involve people in local democracy.

125940.explanation: When you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, wonderful things happen. The municipality should not hinder this but encourage it. Our Adopt Green motion is one example of this.

125942.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to earn money with their farms. A store selling good products from local land or a nice campsite can help with this. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

125944.explanation: There are already too many distribution centers in Voorne aan Zee. We complete existing plans such as those in Kickersbloem-III, but that's enough. More would damage the landscape and much of the work is done by poorly paid labor migrants. This also protects our small retailers.

125945.explanation: Asylum seekers should be reasonably accommodated by all municipalities. The Distribution Law provides the right tools for this, and small-scale asylum reception in Voorne aan Zee would be appropriate. If this is not acted upon, expensive emergency reception will unnecessarily continue.

125946.explanation: It has been shown in many places that linking housing to work can lead to inhumane effects and even homelessness. When labor migrants, including seasonal workers, are involved, society must work with entrepreneurs to provide suitable housing.

125947.explanation: The Netherlands is a densely populated country; we must properly protect the green spaces we have. Green is essential for cooling, clean air, and a pleasant neighborhood. Animals and other plants cannot do without trees. Therefore: plant a new tree for every tree cut down. GroenLinks-PvdA introduced a motion about this.

125951.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other rubbish on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is to collect bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and results in cleaner streets.

125953.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. Yet many important places are not adequately accessible, for example, for wheelchair users. Where entrepreneurs cannot easily afford such adjustments, the municipality should assist.

125954.explanation: We find this statement too broad and general. More generally, this option should also be offered to private landowners, for example, for informal care homes in the garden. GroenLinks-PvdA supported a motion in that sense.

125955.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction and keep you healthy. But for people receiving social assistance, sports are often too expensive. In our view, the municipality should ensure that this group can join a sports club for free.

125956.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own environment, from the street to the municipal council. Many local media struggle to stay afloat, and the municipality should contribute, as GroenLinks-PvdA has stated in its election program.

125958.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

125959.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

125963.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we usually do not think more cameras are the right solution. Your privacy is a major asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the streets who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

125964.explanation: A women's shelter is sometimes extremely important. There is one in Spijkenisse, and as a regional facility, that is sufficient. Sometimes being slightly further away is even preferable.

125968.explanation: This is inappropriate use of holiday homes, while Voorne aan Zee is a tourist area. Also, holiday homes are often not designed for permanent residence.

125969.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the greenery around our place of residence as well as possible. However, the current housing shortage requires extra measures, so within reasonable limits we must allow this.

125970.explanation: Generally agreed, but with the caveat that it is not always possible. In that case, it must not be enforced because that causes more uncertainty and new problems. The municipality must also place trust.

125971.explanation: If the waiver of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will face major problems. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water. Therefore, we are not automatically in favor of waivers but do support them on a case-by-case basis.

125974.explanation: The availability of electric charging points should not be an obstacle to choosing an electric car, so more electric charging stations must be promoted.

125976.explanation: This will not play much of a role in the large health centers, but all the more so in home and nursing care. People who need care are extra vulnerable and must feel optimally 'at home'.

125979.explanation: There is no need to lower it, but in light of VAT increases and competitive effects, the tourist tax should not be raised. It should also correspond to that of comparable municipalities.

125985.explanation: Voorne aan Zee currently has two legal coffee shops due to the ongoing cannabis experiment that GroenLinks-PvdA strongly supports. Until this experiment has been evaluated, we need to hold back.

125987.explanation: Limited high-rise buildings are acceptable in the cores of Voorne aan Zee due to the current housing shortage. So no skyscrapers, but taller residential flats that fit within the environmental plan.

125988.explanation: Renting out individual rooms is a basic recipe for housing large groups of labor migrants. Subdividing homes harms livability in a neighborhood due to higher occupancy concentration, increased noise nuisance, parking problems, and waste. It also often leads to unjustifiably higher rents.

125989.explanation: Where possible, we must use the available space more intelligently. In general, we are against creating more parking spaces in the cores, but if other solutions are exhausted, this may be unavoidable.

125992.explanation: It is located near Kickersbloem-III, so access should go via the industrial area. This relieves traffic pressure through the center of Hellevoetsluis.

125995.explanation: We support the implementation of the two planned new turbines near the Haringvliet dam, replacing the current five (or six?) small turbines. Only if these meet the standards according to the NEVELE ruling, even if it concerns fewer than three turbines.

125938.explanation: We prefer a combination of men and women in the municipal executive. The important thing is that the most suitable people are chosen.

125939.explanation: Complex issues cannot be captured in a simple question. There are alternatives such as citizen panels or polls, consultation evenings, citizen assemblies, and neighborhood or area discussions.

125940.explanation: Residents are sufficiently involved and passionate to do this.

125942.explanation: We see this already happens a lot in Voorne aan Zee. It stimulates the local economy and the tourism sector.

125944.explanation: New local businesses should primarily be sustainable and innovative and provide additional employment.

125945.explanation: Subject to existing agreements and legal obligations.

125946.explanation: These employers are required to provide good housing for their employees (good employment practices). In practice, this often involves buying homes in neighborhoods. Providing housing on their own premises reduces pressure on housing in the neighborhoods.

125947.explanation: Felling means replanting: this way we ensure our living environment remains green and healthy.

125951.explanation: The collection of bulky waste remains a paid service from the municipality, as it is now.

125953.explanation: Accessibility for people with disabilities is very important to us. Residents, entrepreneurs, associations, and the municipality all contribute to this.

125954.explanation: It is important that the polder remains open. Caregiver and family homes on private property should be allowed.

125955.explanation: Sports contribute to health, relaxation, and participation. That is why we focus on good facilities and equal opportunities for everyone.

125956.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives money from the municipality annually. Additionally, the broadcaster, like any other association or foundation, can apply for extra subsidies.

125958.explanation: Music in the classroom is currently funded by a subsidy. We wholeheartedly support this.

125959.explanation: We want to maintain space for diversity in activities and appreciation for both large and small social associations. Residents, entrepreneurs, associations, and the municipality all contribute to this.

125963.explanation: We ensure everyone feels safe on the streets. We promote this through enforcement and the (re)design of public spaces, for example by adequate lighting and/or camera surveillance.

125964.explanation: This is arranged locally with surrounding municipalities. We will expand this if necessary.

125968.explanation: Vacation homes must remain vacation homes.

125969.explanation: In Hellevoetsluis and Brielle, we are starting to build outside the existing village boundaries.

125970.explanation: Participation and combating poverty often start with having a job. We will continue to strive for maximum reintegration of people receiving benefits.

125971.explanation: We believe that families with low incomes should be supported as much as possible through various schemes.

125974.explanation: For CDA Voorne aan Zee, sustainability starts with responsibility. We want to leave the world better than we found it. The municipality must take the lead in this.

125979.explanation: Taxes will be raised annually by no more than inflation. Last year, the tourist tax was increased by more than inflation.

125985.explanation: The municipality already has two coffee shops. That is sufficient.

125987.explanation: High-rise residential flats like those in Rotterdam or Spijkenisse do not fit the character of the residential cores of Voorne aan Zee.

125988.explanation: Subdividing homes leads to too many tenants on one plot. This can more easily lead to nuisance.

125992.explanation: To relieve the passage through Hellevoetsluis in that way.

125995.explanation: Two are already being added at the Haringvliet dam. More are not necessary.

125938.explanation: The best person in the right place, based on talent, passion, and craftsmanship. That is in the interest of the municipality. It does not matter whether this person is male or female.

125939.explanation: With the new participation policy, many opportunities have been added for our residents: citizen initiatives, the right to challenge, and participation at all policy levels. Formal referenda can be an addition to gather information.

125940.explanation: Residents know their neighborhood best and feel more involved when they are in charge themselves. We support self-management where possible, possibly with support from the municipality.

125942.explanation: We advocate that farmers can undertake initiatives on or near their property to supplement their business operations, such as a roadside sales point, a shop, or a B&B. Examples include the Ice Cream Lady in Rockanje or the nature campsite Zonnehoeve in Oudenhoorn.

125944.explanation: Distribution warehouses take up a lot of space, are visible in the landscape, generate much extra traffic, and contribute little to local employment. We want to focus on meaningful employment for our residents and preserving the landscape and environment.

125945.explanation: We do not want a large-scale asylum seekers center, tent camp, or flex park. We do support shelter for those who truly need it at a humane level. This shelter should provide prospects for a full life, integration, community involvement, and employment without displacing our own residents.

125946.explanation: Seasonal workers are often people who have worked for the same employer for years and need housing for just a few months. Currently, homes are being taken from the stock available to our own residents. We want to prevent this.

125947.explanation: To preserve our environment and landscape, nature must be compensated for what disappears due to human activities. This means replanting trees and greenery in general, at least one-for-one.

125951.explanation: Nothing is free, but high-quality services, such as bulky waste collection, contribute to circular waste streams and the circular crafts center. By focusing on clean waste streams, opportunities arise for the economy and employment.

125953.explanation: We support helping entrepreneurs to make our environment even more beautiful. This does not necessarily have to come from municipal subsidies but can also be part of regional, national, or European initiatives, such as a Regional Deal.

125954.explanation: We support allowing opportunities for additional building on farm properties, such as houses or small-scale residential forms. However, our landscape must remain green and building on agricultural land cannot be done arbitrarily.

125955.explanation: We find it important that sports are accessible and affordable for everyone. Currently, provisions exist through schemes like the child package. Our goal is to organize indoor and outdoor sports so that individual support is not necessary.

125956.explanation: National politics and legislation cause our local identity to diminish and reduce local input for our environment. This also applies to the local broadcaster. We believe our local identity is worth preserving.

125958.explanation: We already do this and want to continue doing so.

125959.explanation: We are absolutely in favor of sports and clubs. They make our municipality beautiful through cooperation, club life, and social cohesion. Every euro invested returns 2.73 euros in social value.

125963.explanation: The feeling of safety is under pressure, both nationally and in our municipality. Police tasks are disappearing and capacity is decreasing. The use of camera surveillance at strategic locations to combat nuisance or prevent burglaries is acceptable.

125964.explanation: Within the municipality, there are several facilities for shelter. We believe these facilities should be handled regionally. This approach yields the greatest financial benefits and protection for those involved. We focus on prevention and early detection.

125968.explanation: Not all holiday homes are suitable for permanent residence and often these homes do not benefit our own residents. Custom solutions or selection are needed, for example for residents of Cita Romana in Hellevoetsluis. They are perfectly suitable as a home address.

125969.explanation: It is essential for our villages to be able to build outside the existing village boundaries. This benefits village residents as well as the livability of the villages. The current position of the province means we must apply high-rise buildings, which we do not want in our villages.

125970.explanation: We believe it is important to give people a helping hand and support when necessary. At the same time, the demand for personnel has never been greater. We believe that if you can work, it is also your responsibility, and you cannot live indefinitely on benefits.

125971.explanation: We find it important to keep municipal charges as low as possible so they are affordable for everyone, including middle incomes. Waivers or discounts are not necessarily reserved for income but for individual circumstances.

125974.explanation: Parking in our villages and fortress is under pressure. Removing parking spaces for charging points is illogical. The government is again giving more space for gasoline cars, and charging technology is evolving to a 20-minute charge. Parking spaces are for everyone.

125976.explanation: Everyone is free to choose their own healthcare provider.

125979.explanation: We want a reasonable tourist tax that benefits local facilities. Part of the tourist tax should be used by the sector itself. We want a tax that provides long-term clarity and certainty to entrepreneurs.

125985.explanation: In Voorne aan Zee, we have the legal option for two coffeeshops. That is our maximum. We do not want coffeeshops in the small villages, around shopping centers, or near our schools.

125987.explanation: We do not want tall residential towers in our villages and neighborhoods. However, we see opportunities for buildings up to four or five stories in the larger villages. Hellevoetsluis could develop more like a city, where residential towers are possible.

125988.explanation: We want to invest in rental homes and home ownership. Renting rooms is a temporary solution that is often expensive for tenants and offers little future perspective. The balance and livability in the neighborhood is an important consideration when deciding whether to allow room rental.

125989.explanation: We support good parking in the villages without too much trouble. At the same time, we do not want to remove public green spaces or facilities to allow an extra parking space. We do support extra parking spaces based on smart and innovative solutions.

125992.explanation: Farm Frites is a highly valued entrepreneur and employer; therefore, we want to invest in alternative options to retain Farm Frites, improve accessibility, and ensure the livability of the area.

125995.explanation: We have advocated for several years for a different approach regarding wind turbines in our area. We see possibilities for offshore wind turbines but not on land. Wind turbines are a profit model for energy companies and developers at the expense of residents.

125938.explanation: Leaders are elected based on quality, experience, and decisiveness. Gender quotas distract from good governance and cause inequality rather than equality.

125939.explanation: Residents must have direct say in major decisions. A local (binding) referendum strengthens democracy and ensures that the voice of residents truly counts.

125940.explanation: Local involvement leads to better management and more ownership. The municipality must facilitate residents’ initiatives and not block them with unnecessary rules.

125942.explanation: Farmers must be given space to make their businesses future-proof. Secondary activities provide extra income and strengthen the local economy, provided they fit in the landscape.

125944.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers lead to the spoiling of the landscape, extra traffic pressure, and few local jobs. The character of Voorne aan Zee must be protected.

125945.explanation: Voorne aan Zee is full. Safety, livability, and housing shortage for our own residents take priority. Asylum reception is a national task and should not be offloaded onto our municipality.

125946.explanation: Employers are responsible for their staff. This prevents pressure on residential neighborhoods and maintains livability for residents.

125947.explanation: Green is essential for a healthy living environment. Unnecessary tree felling must be prevented, and where trees are lost, this must be compensated as much as possible by replanting.

125951.explanation: Waste collection is a core municipal task. Free collection prevents litter and ensures a clean living environment.

125953.explanation: Accessibility is important but should not be a financial penalty for entrepreneurs. Targeted support from the municipality is reasonable.

125954.explanation: Farmers know their land and surroundings. Small-scale housing for family or personal use helps alleviate the housing shortage without damaging the landscape.

125955.explanation: Sport is important, but financial support must go hand in hand with personal responsibility and participation. Municipal policy should focus on involvement and progression, not automatic reimbursements.

125956.explanation: Blindly subsidizing is not a solution. Local media do play an important role in highlighting local news and democratic oversight. Therefore, the municipality must handle support responsibly and not cut back on it.

125958.explanation: Culture and music are valuable, but parents and schools also have their own responsibility in this.

125959.explanation: Sports clubs fulfill an important social function. Temporary support helps keep clubs afloat for more than just sports.

125963.explanation: Targeted use of camera surveillance can help tackle nuisance and crime. The privacy of residents must be protected, and camera surveillance must not lead to mass surveillance or a feeling of permanent monitoring.

125964.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be helped quickly and safely. Protection takes precedence over bureaucracy.

125968.explanation: Permanent residence offers a direct solution to housing shortages and prevents vacancies.

125969.explanation: The open landscape and village characters must be preserved. First utilize what already exists.

125970.explanation: Welfare benefits are not an end point. Participation strengthens self-worth and society.

125971.explanation: People with low incomes must not be further squeezed by municipal charges. Waivers prevent problematic debts and provide peace of mind, while poverty policy should remain focused on enabling people to find work.

125974.explanation: Parking pressure must not increase further due to climate policy. Electric driving must never be at the expense of accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs.

125976.explanation: Care must be accessible to everyone, professional, and of high quality. Identity or personal beliefs should never take precedence over the quality, effectiveness, and safety of care.

125979.explanation: Tourism and recreation are important for the local economy. Higher taxes harm entrepreneurs.

125985.explanation: Policy should focus on controlling nuisance and crime. Automatically blocking coffeeshops is not an end in itself. Livability and safety are. The municipality must be able to assess responsibly on a case-by-case basis.

125987.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit the character of Voorne aan Zee and harm village landscapes. Lack of space must not lead to massive high-rises. Small-scale apartments (maximum five floors) can contribute to solving the housing shortage and improving flow for local residents.

125988.explanation: Subdivision into small rooms puts pressure on livability and safety in residential neighborhoods and increases the chance of overcrowding and malpractices. Housing must primarily remain available to local residents seeking a full-fledged home to live in.

125989.explanation: Accessibility is essential for residents, entrepreneurs, and the elderly.

125992.explanation: Accessibility is important, but major infrastructure projects must be carefully weighed in terms of usefulness, costs, and impact on the environment.

125995.explanation: The same applies here: no wind turbines on land. Protect the living environment and listen to residents.

125938.explanation: We believe the new executive board should mainly focus on cooperation and that each alderman brings substantive knowledge. The preference is for a diverse and inclusive board.

125939.explanation: For important topics, we opt for a citizens' assembly. Residents who have knowledge and/or a stake should be able to think along and participate in discussions about important subjects to reach well-considered decisions.

125940.explanation: The municipality could play a supervisory role here and set conditions on the management.

125942.explanation: In Voorne aan Zee we are fortunate that there are still farmers producing food. We are proud of that. Due to regulations, farmers invest heavily in their operations and have little future perspective. We would like to offer farmers the opportunity to generate extra income.

125944.explanation: The municipality is insufficiently accessible to allow more transport movements over the tight infrastructure.

125945.explanation: The municipality must set conditions on the scale and manner of facilitating asylum reception that respect residents' interests and sufficiently guarantee safety. The municipality should communicate and consult with residents and stakeholders in a timely manner.

125946.explanation: Seasonal agricultural laborers are desperately needed in farming and horticulture. Housing them in town centers often causes undesirable nuisance and takes up available housing.

125947.explanation: The municipality has a green structural plan. Tree-felling policy belongs in this plan as well. In some places this is very relevant. In new residential areas, the creation of parks could be chosen.

125951.explanation: We prefer regular free bulky waste collection on fixed dates at collection points.

125953.explanation: People with disabilities make up an important part of our population. Each situation must be examined to what extent the municipality can and should financially contribute to making a building more accessible for people with disabilities.

125954.explanation: There are still many complications here because building land is more expensive than agricultural land. If it contributes to solving the housing shortage and keeping youth in Voorne aan Zee, it is a realistic option.

125955.explanation: If people with welfare benefits have ended up in this situation through no fault of their own, or are hindered by regulations from being able to work, the municipality may contribute to that.

125956.explanation: The municipality can choose to purchase services from the local broadcaster. Broadcasters have other ways to generate income and obtain subsidies.

125958.explanation: Every primary school should be able to offer music lessons, whether with subsidy or without.

125959.explanation: Sports clubs are an important part of the welfare and health of our residents. Many families struggle to pay the fees, which negatively affects the sports clubs.

125963.explanation: At locations where it adds value to ensure safety, camera surveillance should definitely be used. We are absolutely against using cameras to track or monitor people.

125964.explanation: With the recurring cases of femicide and hatred against women in the country, every municipality should be able to provide a shelter for victims of domestic violence, including Voorne aan Zee.

125968.explanation: This must be made possible for homes that comply with the building code. For locations with recreational homes and caravans, each home must be assessed to ensure it meets standards, such as fire regulations.

125969.explanation: The municipality must have a vision and develop with a future-oriented approach. Multiple scenarios must be worked out to clarify the usefulness and necessity for the long term. This is especially useful now for the small communities.

125970.explanation: Every person depends on meaning in life. Participation in society is an important part of that. Everyone wants to matter, even when someone receives welfare benefits.

125971.explanation: Many residents emigrate due to the enormous regulatory and tax burden in the Netherlands. The municipality must clearly understand the causes that lead residents to require waivers.

125974.explanation: However, this must be done gradually, in connection with developments in electric mobility in Voorne aan Zee. Not arbitrarily more, but designated sustainably and purposefully.

125976.explanation: People who need care should be offered care. When this care does not align with their religion, culture, or beliefs, it is an individual choice not to make use of the care offered.

125979.explanation: To make Voorne aan Zee more attractive to tourists, a reduction in tourist tax is desirable. Tourism is an important source of income for our local economy.

125985.explanation: If new coffeeshops do not lead to more nuisance, they could be permitted under conditions and supervision. ONS Voorne prefers regular, controlled coffeeshops rather than dealing on the street.

125987.explanation: The architecture is characteristic for the image of Voorne aan Zee. High residential flats do not fit this image. Also, there is insufficient demonstrated need for them.

125988.explanation: In situations where it provides an appropriate solution, the municipality should allow this.

125989.explanation: A sensible parking policy must be developed, including the available parking spaces within the town centers. This is related to other parking facilities, accessibility, including public transport access to institutions, amenities, residences, and shops in the centers.

125992.explanation: The municipality must primarily develop a mobility vision that prioritizes infrastructure in Voorne aan Zee. The so-called 'patatweg' can be part of this, as well as a ring road to Kickersbloem, a good emergency route, access to new residential areas, etc.

125995.explanation: ONS Voorne will oppose all plans to install more wind turbines.

125938.explanation: The gender of an alderman does not matter, as everyone is equal; what matters are their competencies. It would be nice if the ratio were 50/50.

125939.explanation: Residents must always be able to express their opinions and have the freedom to determine what is important, but a referendum can raise expectations that may not be fulfilled.

125940.explanation: Under certain circumstances and conditions, residents can be involved in managing municipal facilities and (partly) take over this role. Of course, this must include conditions ensuring safety, continuity, and accessibility of amenities.

125942.explanation: A sales point for the yield of own land should always be allowed, but permitting a campsite must be assessed based on a license application, under conditions and considering the circumstances.

125944.explanation: The labor demanded in distribution centers adds no value to the municipality; rather, it is a burden in the form of labor migration, possibly putting extra pressure on the housing market and overloading infrastructure with additional traffic. The Maasvlakte is better suited for this.

125945.explanation: The temporary asylum boat does not meet the provision standards that we want our municipality to represent. We want a good structural solution in line with the Dispersion Act.

125946.explanation: It must explicitly concern seasonal labor. If, besides for example students, laborers from abroad or outside the region are needed, the employer must provide (short-term) housing on their own site. There must be conditions and supervision by authorities.

125947.explanation: If the owner of the felled tree (felling permit) is unable to do so, the municipality will take care of it.

125951.explanation: Not everyone is able to bring their bulky waste to the municipal depot. Free collection prevents littering. Savings can then be made on the costs of removing litter.

125953.explanation: We consider inclusivity very important, but the entrepreneur has the freedom to make their own choice based on commercial and/or social considerations and must bear the costs themselves. Municipal subsidies might fit within a broader policy.

125954.explanation: Using the 'space for space' scheme, building land is required. Not only for farmers but for all larger lots, it should be possible to add a (pre-)caregiver dwelling.

125955.explanation: Certainly all children should be able to join a sports club, but the group for whom this is not feasible goes beyond those receiving social assistance. A sports fund that people can appeal to is a good idea in consultation with sports clubs.

125956.explanation: Support is important to ensure independent journalism, and a quality boost is needed for this.

125958.explanation: School education should cover more than just knowledge transfer. Cultural development is also important.

125959.explanation: This depends on the type of sport, membership numbers, social impact, and diversity in the sports offerings. Special attention should be given to sufficient diversity and grassroots sports in Voorne aan Zee.

125963.explanation: According to D66, current camera surveillance is adequate. If more is needed, it should be possible, for example in parks. The principle is no, unless.

125964.explanation: Currently, it is regulated locally and that is sufficient, but if it turns out more is needed, of course it should be expanded.

125968.explanation: But temporarily. Here too, the holiday park operators in the market sector have an important role. Exorbitant speculation must be prevented.

125969.explanation: Living outside existing village boundaries has pros and cons that residents can weigh themselves. Moreover, village boundaries can fade or be adjusted based on this or other developments.

125970.explanation: Only if this is meaningful and truly possible, viewed from the client’s perspective.

125971.explanation: If exemption from municipal taxes can be used to combat poverty within the municipality, this must remain possible.

125974.explanation: The system on request works well. Furthermore, attention must be paid to grid overload. Solutions exist, which Voorne aan Zee could also apply.

125976.explanation: It would be nice if it were feasible. However, the reality is completely different. Being able to get the help you need is more important.

125979.explanation: Tourist tax must fit within the limits that apply to comparable municipalities. Increasing revenue by raising taxes should not be the starting point.

125985.explanation: Legal cannabis trade is ordinary trade. The market will self-regulate. However, when issuing permits, great attention must be paid to the environment and the nature of entrepreneurship locally. Clear conditions must be set to prevent nuisance.

125987.explanation: Of course, fitting in with the surroundings. Especially for seniors and starters.

125988.explanation: Owner-occupation will contribute to solving the housing shortage. Allowing renting out rooms will make renting more financially attractive again, which may reduce owner-occupation and jeopardize tenants' living conditions and safety.

125989.explanation: If it is possible through smarter design of outdoor spaces in the town centers, without losing green areas.

125992.explanation: This should have existed long ago.

125995.explanation: But what is meant by 'new'? We adhere to the agreements made. If there are new plans or new applications for new locations, the municipality must consider them and only then decide to consult or oppose.

125938.explanation: Mayors and aldermen should be selected based on content, not gender, origin, or similar criteria.

125939.explanation: Too many decisions are currently made by the council and coalition parties. Residents should be able to exercise more influence.

125940.explanation: This should be possible under certain conditions, but it does not apply to everything.

125942.explanation: Absolutely, to support and promote the local economy.

125944.explanation: It is good for employment, but on the other hand, traffic safety and accessibility decrease.

125945.explanation: This comes at the expense of safety, social services, housing for those looking for homes, and so on. Sending a signal to the province is also good. We do not accept everything.

125946.explanation: Must is a strong word, but this can be possible under certain conditions, also due to a shortage of staff in this sector.

125951.explanation: It is never free; we already pay for it. If this is not taxed extra, the street scene will not improve. It is already often dumped carelessly.

125953.explanation: To a limited extent, this is possible. We would like to see the money then returning to the municipality and the taxpayer.

125955.explanation: Other organizations are already providing support.

125956.explanation: The local broadcaster must be independent.

125958.explanation: Schools should include this as a standard part of their curriculum. This can be done with one hour per week. It can possibly be done with a limited contribution.

125959.explanation: In principle, disagree, but this completely depends on the situation. Contributions are already being made in various ways.

125963.explanation: Big Brother is already watching enough.

125964.explanation: There are no indications that this is necessary.

125969.explanation: The rural landscape must also be protected. This also applies to wind turbines outside village boundaries.

125971.explanation: In principle yes, but the risk is that this group ends up in financial trouble, which costs society (the municipality) even more.

125976.explanation: People should be grateful that help is offered.

125979.explanation: Tourism is good for local entrepreneurs.

125987.explanation: Absolutely not. There is one, and that is already one too many.

125988.explanation: Under certain conditions, this should be made possible.

125989.explanation: We are not lacking parking spaces. If problems arise in the future, then yes. For regular cars.

125992.explanation: The trucks from Farm Frites cause a lot of nuisance. But this should not be paid for with taxpayers' money.

125995.explanation: Absolutely. Also one of our positions. We tried to stop the new wind turbines at the Haringvlietdam. Unfortunately, this was already decided in the previous council period.

125938.explanation: We focus on quality in people. Good knowledge of the portfolios is an important starting point for us.

125939.explanation: Participation with and by residents is an important part around major topics. A referendum can be part of this. We are not in favor of a referendum on a single statement presented to residents.

125940.explanation: This is already applied in the municipality. With new initiatives, good agreements are important to ensure sustainability in the long term.

125942.explanation: Customization, encourage consultation between the agricultural and tourism sectors for coordination. We review applications critically and they can definitely be an addition to the municipality.

125944.explanation: There are already enough large warehouses with distribution centers in our municipality. The focus should be on small-scale and local entrepreneurs.

125945.explanation: Realistically, this is not possible and we cannot realize it as a municipality. We are bound by the Spreading Act. Echt voor Voorne stands for humane policy, and a participation process with residents will start soon.

125946.explanation: This frees up homes in residential neighborhoods. Employers can also choose a central solution such as a labor migrant hotel or seek cooperation among themselves to realize something.

125947.explanation: Echt voor Voorne finds it important to replace felled trees one-to-one.

125951.explanation: Echt voor Voorne is keen to reduce waste dumping. If we, as a municipality, for example by collecting free of charge four times a year per address, can achieve this, it is a good option.

125953.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneur to finance this. As a municipality, we can at most take on an advisory role.

125954.explanation: We want to make this possible under conditions; it requires customization.

125955.explanation: There is already support from the municipality. Not tied to benefits, but to income. Families that need support can already receive it. These families should be approached specifically. Support not only in sports, but also in culture among others.

125956.explanation: The local broadcaster is making good progress in reporting and visibility among residents. We find the current budget sufficient for now.

125958.explanation: Music for the Classroom must continue to be supported, and where possible, we also support activities outside of education.

125959.explanation: Subject to conditions, we are certainly in favor of this; however, associations must be well managed so that we only assist associations that have genuinely gotten into trouble through no fault of their own.

125963.explanation: It increases the feeling of safety and cameras should be placed at hotspots throughout the entire municipality. Installing cameras does not oblige us to refrain from introducing or scaling up other measures to improve safety.

125964.explanation: Echt voor Voorne supports this-a good shelter for our own residents who need it.

125968.explanation: It is part of the broader housing problem. This measure can partly solve it.

125969.explanation: Building should also be possible outside village boundaries. Customized approaches are required for each area.

125970.explanation: It is good if we can help someone on welfare advance towards paid employment. Given the diversity of reasons why residents are on welfare, this requires a tailored approach.

125971.explanation: The municipality already does this and it should continue this way.

125974.explanation: Residents can submit applications, and the municipality assesses each request and meets the needs of our residents.

125976.explanation: This complicates and makes arranging care more expensive. The choice of care providers lies with the insurers.

125979.explanation: Echt voor Voorne does not want to lower this tax and prefers long-term agreements with the sector.

125985.explanation: The two current coffeeshops are sufficient; we do not support the addition of more shops.

125987.explanation: Apartment buildings provide a solution to the housing shortage. However, good agreements must be made about what is considered 'high'. Not every construction site is suitable for this.

125988.explanation: Echt voor Voorne sees no local need for this. There is a chance that these homes would mainly be occupied by migrant workers.

125989.explanation: Villages must not become nothing but parking lots.

125992.explanation: Echt voor Voorne does not see this road as the only part of the solution to accessibility. This requires a broader approach.

125995.explanation: We will not support new applications.

125938.explanation: Aim for an equal number. Of course, selection must be based on quality!

125940.explanation: But ownership remains with the municipality.

125942.explanation: Only if this does not come at the expense of nature.

125944.explanation: The infrastructure is not sufficient for that.

125945.explanation: If asylum reception is no longer needed, do not rush to close the reception location as quickly as possible. As long as the Distribution Act remains in effect, the municipality must comply with it.

125946.explanation: First resolve abuses in the housing of seasonal workers. Set a maximum number and do not allow them to stay longer than the season.

125947.explanation: But it would be better not to cut down trees.

125951.explanation: But this must be collected quickly by appointment to prevent littering.

125954.explanation: The municipality determines where houses are built and for whom. Otherwise, inequality based on the land price will arise.

125956.explanation: This money should be used to make traditional media accessible to more people.

125958.explanation: But income-dependent, so that children whose parents cannot afford it still have the chance to learn to make music.

125959.explanation: Associations should manage their own finances properly with assistance from national federations.

125963.explanation: We do not believe that more cameras automatically mean more safety. Structural measures are needed for that. Between cameras and enforcement officers, enforcement officers are preferred.

125964.explanation: The exact implementation must be carefully considered.

125968.explanation: Those homes must then be used for habitation and for people who need them.

125969.explanation: First address vacancy and build socially.

125970.explanation: The municipality must help people move from welfare to work with income. Only if that truly does not succeed can other activities help keep them connected to society.

125971.explanation: People who already have little should not be burdened further.

125974.explanation: Let residents decide based on needs.

125976.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is a great value.

125979.explanation: Tourism is an important source of income.

125985.explanation: There are already two. First ensure that no health risks and nuisance arise around them.

125987.explanation: However, not building up the entire coast and keeping houses affordable. Only for residential use, no holiday apartments.

125988.explanation: Splitting homes should also be possible. Not a solution for labor migrants who then pay full rent!

125989.explanation: Let residents decide based on needs.

125992.explanation: Reduces nuisance and insecurity in residential neighborhoods.

125995.explanation: Searching for better locations, for example along the Haringvlietdam.

125938.explanation: For us, the quality of aldermen is more important than gender.

125939.explanation: It is important that residents can express their vote not only once every four years, but also intermittently on important issues.

125940.explanation: Management requires professional knowledge and continuity, while initiatives often rely on a small group of volunteers. At the same time, the municipality remains ultimately responsible. An exception is the autonomy of a sports club.

125942.explanation: We will always encourage the opening of a farm shop. For a campsite, careful consideration must be given to how it fits into the environment. Moreover, there is a big difference between a mini-campsite with a small number of overnight spots and a large-scale campsite.

125944.explanation: We are against further urbanization of Voorne aan Zee. Our infrastructure can no longer handle that either.

125945.explanation: At this moment, we have an asylum ship in Hellevoetsluis, where local residents have been experiencing considerable (noise) nuisance for quite some time. This asylum boat must leave as soon as possible. For us, no asylum seekers' center is desired in Voorne aan Zee.

125946.explanation: We are positive about small-scale housing of our own employees on our own land, provided it is stipulated that only our own employees are housed there, so it does not become commercial.

125947.explanation: Every disappearing tree must be compensated so that the total number of trees remains at least stable.

125951.explanation: We believe that bulky waste should definitely be collected free of charge at home, but with clear conditions.

125953.explanation: We consider an inclusive society extremely important. If a financial incentive motivates entrepreneurs to make society even more inclusive by improving accessibility, we support that. Of course, it is not the case that the municipality will pay for everything.

125954.explanation: Yes, if it concerns an own home, a home for first-degree family members, or small-scale housing for own seasonal workers with clear conditions. No, if it leads to uncontrolled housing construction and erosion of the rural area.

125955.explanation: Being able to play sports is extremely important, but we prefer to introduce a sports pass that offers financial compensation for sports up to a certain amount. We would like to use this for people up to 130 percent of the social assistance standard.

125956.explanation: We do not see extra funding as something that should be taken for granted. If the local broadcaster submits a substantiated request for extra money from the municipality, it will be critically reviewed and weighed each time to determine whether it is justified in our opinion.

125958.explanation: Artistic orientation such as music, drawing and crafts are compulsory subjects in primary school, so there must be sufficient financial resources for this.

125959.explanation: The municipality must be careful with sports clubs and step in if necessary, except in cases of mismanagement at a club.

125963.explanation: Camera surveillance should not be a goal but an ultimate means to improve, for example, safety in a particular area.

125964.explanation: A women's shelter in Voorne aan Zee should be seen as part of a regional facility: shelter is provided in interaction with other municipalities, ensuring safety, anonymity and continuity.

125968.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in privately owned recreational homes, because this utilizes existing housing and helps alleviate the housing shortage. This does not apply to holiday parks, where the recreational function must remain paramount.

125969.explanation: Building outside village boundaries should not be the norm, but the last step after all realistic options within the village boundaries have been explored. Conditions are that there are sufficient facilities, infrastructure is well organized and that housing is for our own residents.

125970.explanation: Meaningful social work as a reciprocal obligation for social assistance benefits can contribute to a social network and easier return to paid work.

125971.explanation: Stopping the remittance waiver scheme, in our view, does not help reduce the financial problems of our residents but will have the opposite effect.

125974.explanation: The number of parking spaces is already quite limited. Of course, there must be enough charging stations available, but not at the expense of spaces for petrol cars.

125976.explanation: Care is already under enormous pressure; this will only get worse if distinctions are made based on faith, culture, or religious belief.

125979.explanation: An increase is undesirable because we see a stacking of burdens: higher VAT, energy prices and costs.

125985.explanation: We consider two coffeeshops sufficient within our municipality.

125987.explanation: Although building smaller homes is absolutely necessary, we base consenting to apartment buildings on location and number of floors. The building must fit into the environment, which often results in a maximum number of stories.

125988.explanation: Loose room rentals are unmanageable, increase parking pressure, and cause single-family homes to disappear because renting rooms separately yields more money.

125989.explanation: New parking spaces near existing buildings are not desirable, but the parking standard for new construction should increase to a realistic number. Cars remain welcome.

125992.explanation: We cannot agree to building the Patatweg if we have no idea of its route, whether people/businesses will be affected, and what neighborhood participation has yielded. The situation has changed in recent years due to the construction of Kickersbloem 3.

125995.explanation: Wind turbines (on land) are outdated and should no longer be allowed in our municipality.

125938.explanation: We prefer to see as many women as possible in politics. The executive has a role model function, and women often have a different perspective on issues such as public space and the balance between economy and welfare.

125939.explanation: LEF supports 'preferenda', which offer more choices, not just a yes-no or for-against. The phrasing can be very suggestive or even confusing.

125940.explanation: Sometimes residents have great ideas for their neighborhood or can take over a pub or sports hall. It's wonderful when the community takes responsibility for the city’s care. Citizen participation improves the city and should definitely receive a budget.

125942.explanation: Farmers are entrepreneurs dependent on various branches of activity to generate a stable income. Recreation is part of that.

125944.explanation: Distribution centers have little economic value and cause a lot of nuisance. We prefer to use our scarce land for agriculture, nature, and housing!

125945.explanation: Every municipality must take a fair share of refugees. By doing this, the nuisance at overcrowded asylum centers also decreases.

125946.explanation: It is logical that companies that make extensive use of guest workers provide accommodation. However, we do not want shady practices and exorbitant room prices that are deducted from wages. The labor inspectorate must strictly supervise the living conditions and fire safety.

125947.explanation: LEF supports a mandatory tree planting policy. Plant first, then cut down.

125951.explanation: LEF wants to encourage residents to dispose of their bulky waste. Free collection makes this more attractive. At the same time, we want to stimulate the reuse of goods, among other things by having repair cafés and community workshops in every neighborhood where items can get a second life.

125953.explanation: Entrepreneurs may be willing, but often do not know how. The municipality can organize information afternoons or let entrepreneurs experience what it is like to have a disability through role-playing. This lowers the barrier to engage with this important policy.

125954.explanation: Inheritance of land shares is a great way to combat the housing shortage. Farmers can earn extra income and home seekers can give a boost to the countryside.

125955.explanation: Sports are extremely important to stay physically and mentally healthy. Being a member of a club increases the chance to meet people and find an (unpaid) job. It also saves healthcare costs, so it is a good investment!

125956.explanation: Reliable and independent journalism is crucial for democracy. We want to promote this. We also want to safeguard the quality of broadcasters.

125958.explanation: Music education is good for brain development and developing skills that contribute to good school results. Besides that, learning music is also fun!

125959.explanation: Sports are extremely important to stay physically and mentally healthy. Being a member of a club increases the chance to meet people and find an (unpaid) job. It also saves healthcare costs, so it is a good investment!

125963.explanation: Surveillance cameras and the underlying systems are expensive and do not necessarily increase safety. They mainly provide visibility. Furthermore, all residents of Voorne aan Zee have a right to privacy.

125964.explanation: Violence against women is a widespread phenomenon. A women's shelter can prevent femicide and increase women’s safety. Additionally, LEF wants to focus on the emotional upbringing of men. That starts from birth by giving boys just as much space to show emotions.

125968.explanation: It is good that the national government permits the use of holiday parks and allotment garden complexes for housing. The municipality should not oppose this policy by enforcing prohibitions. Be glad that people have a roof over their heads.

125969.explanation: We can make better use of existing buildings and improve housing turnover. We want to do this via the Housing Preference App. Building outside the village boundaries then becomes unnecessary. This way, we do not lose the character of the municipality.

125970.explanation: LEF wants to eliminate all benefits and allowances that discriminate. Instead, we want a basic income paid to everyone, without conditions and without forms to fill out. It is good if people do social work. We prefer to reward rather than oblige.

125971.explanation: Waiving taxes for residents with low income gives them more breathing room and the ability to participate in society. LEF wants to fully solve this problem through a basic income.

125974.explanation: Electrifying parking spots helps to achieve climate goals and improve air quality in the city. For that, accelerated investment in a smart power grid is necessary. LEF prefers shared cars and free public transport even more. That eases the pressure on our municipality and planet!

125976.explanation: On the one hand, people must be ideologically free. Communities are free to organize their own care. On the other hand, this is not a task of the government, which provides secular care. LEF supports the separation of church and state.

125979.explanation: We gladly welcome tourists to Voorne aan Zee. But it does mean extra pressure on facilities and public spaces. Something should be given in return for this. And we do not believe tourists will stay away because of a slightly higher tax.

125985.explanation: There are currently enough coffeeshops. There is nothing wrong with smoking a joint, but we do not need to expand the supply.

125987.explanation: High-rise buildings seem efficient in land use, but they are not. They make public spaces bleak by increasing wind, resulting in fewer people and thus less social control. LEF prefers buildings of a maximum of five floors, adding floors to existing buildings, and banning vacancies.

125988.explanation: A ban on renting out single rooms often leads to fewer student rooms and higher prices. We want to encourage cohabitation through so-called friend contracts, where you do not have to be a couple to share a rental contract. Give young people a place to live as well.

125989.explanation: There are already plenty of cars. LEF wants a clean public space where children can play safely outside. Instead of more parking spots, we want shared cars and free public transport. Let’s reward good behavior and transform parking spaces into pick-your-own gardens and living streets.

125992.explanation: Can't Piet de Bruijne pay that out of his own pocket? Multinationals always dump costs on the community. Transport via waterways is many times more sustainable. Extra roads attract extra traffic. Even with electric motors, tire wear causes additional fine dust and air pollution.

125995.explanation: LEF supports achieving climate goals. This can primarily be done through energy saving, insulation, and investing in solar panels and geothermal energy. If residents benefit by being members of a cooperative and involved early in decision-making, wind energy is certainly an option.

statements.title: The municipality should construct the "patatweg" road to the Farm Frites company in Oudenhoorn.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV camera surveillance in Voorne aan Zee.

titleParts.text: In Voorne aan Zee moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: More high-rise apartment buildings should be allowed in Voorne aan Zee.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
A domestic violence shelter should be established in Voorne aan Zee.

titleParts.text: In Voorne aan Zee moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The residents of Voorne aan Zee should be able to voice their opinion on important matters through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Voorne aan Zee moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should oppose new wind turbines in Hellevoetsluis.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zich verzetten tegen nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
